entries.title: Facebook Ads API Guide from A to Z | Coupler.io Blog
entries.summary:
* Facebook Ads API requires an access token with `ads_read` and `ads_management` permissions.
* Advanced Access permissions are needed to manage other ad accounts.
* Marketing API has rate limiting based on call count within a rolling one-hour window.
* Rate limit calculation: 60 + 400 * Number of Active ads - 0.001 * User Errors.
* Applying for Ads Management Standard Access feature can increase rate limit.
* Facebook Ads API uses Graph API endpoints (nodes + edges) for data retrieval.
* Insights API is used for reporting and retrieving performance data from Facebook marketing campaigns.
* Insights API is available on objects: `/act_{ad-account-id}/insights`, `/{ad-id}/insights`, `/{ad-campaign-id}/insights`, `/{ad-set-id}/insights`.
* Insights API cURL syntax: `https://graph.facebook.com/{api_version}/{ads_object}/insights`.

entries.title: SEO Starter Guide: The Basics | Google Search Central ...
entries.summary:
* SEO stands for Search Engine Optimization, helping search engines understand content and users find a site.

* SEO improvements can take hours to months to be reflected in search results.
* Changes to a website may not always result in noticeable impact in search results.
* Google discovers, crawls, and serves web pages automatically, with most sites being added without manual intervention.
* Waiting a few weeks is recommended to assess the effects of SEO changes on Google Search results.
* Iterating on changes can help if initial results are not satisfactory.
* Checking if Google has already found a site's content is a preliminary step before making SEO changes.
* Add descriptive alt text to images using the `alt` attribute of the `img` element.
* Optimize videos by creating high-quality content and embedding on standalone pages with relevant text.
* Write descriptive text in video titles and description fields.
* Promote website through social media, community engagement, and word of mouth.
* Use offline promotion methods like business cards, letterhead, and newsletters.
* Avoid over-promoting, as it can harm search engine rankings.

* SEO has on-page, technical, off-page, and local components
* SEO ranking factors are crucial for search engine optimization
* Tailored SEO strategies are necessary for different businesses and industries
* SEO experience and expertise are essential for successful implementation
* SEO analytics are necessary to measure and improve SEO results
* SEO content is a key component of a successful SEO strategy
* Technical SEO is a critical aspect of search engine optimization

entries.title: SEO: The Complete Guide for Beginners - Ahrefs
entries.summary:
* Search engines build indexes, rank pages, and personalize results.
* Four main facets of SEO: setting up for success, getting on Google, tracking success, and SEO fundamentals.
* Keyword research involves understanding target customer searches, types of keywords, and prioritizing keywords.
* SEO content creation involves understanding SEO content basics and creating content that ranks.
* On-page SEO involves optimizing pages with target keywords, header tags, title tags, and meta descriptions.
* SEO-friendly URLs, internal links, and external links are important for on-page SEO.
* Image optimization and schema markup can improve search engine understanding.
* User experience and content gaps are important for on-page SEO optimization.
* Tracking on-page SEO performance is crucial for improvement.
* Zero-click search and rich results can be achieved with optimization and schema markup.

entries.title: Search engine optimization - Wikipedia
entries.summary:
* Writing content with frequently searched keyword phrases increases traffic.
* Updating content keeps search engines crawling back, giving additional weight to a site.
* Adding relevant keywords to metadata (title tag, meta description) improves search listings.
* URL canonicalization helps links to different versions of a URL count towards the page's link popularity score.
* White hat SEO techniques conform to search engines' guidelines, involve no deception, and promote accessibility.
* Black hat SEO attempts to improve rankings through disapproved methods, such as hidden text or cloaking.
* Grey hat SEO uses methods that avoid penalties but don't produce the best content for users.
* Search engines may penalize sites using black or grey hat methods.
* 301 redirects can help with URL canonicalization.
* Canonical link element can be used to specify preferred version of a URL.
* Keyword proximity affects search engine ranking.
* Search engines do not pay for organic search traffic.
* Algorithms used by search engines are subject to change.
* No guarantees of continued referrals from search engines.
* Return on investment (ROI) from SEO can be adequate.
* Keyword placement is important for search engine optimization.
* Search engine traffic can be uncertain and unpredictable.
* Businesses relying on search engine traffic are vulnerable to algorithm changes.
* Organic search traffic is free, but rankings are not guaranteed.
